titleStmt.title: SII 3.65: original edition by Eugen Julius Theodor Hultzsch
VI.—INSCRIPTIONS OF KULOTTUNGA-CHOLA I. No. 65.—INSCRIPTION AT TIRUVALANGADU.

summary.p: This inscription (No. 14 of 1896) is engraved on the east wall of the second prākāra of the Vaṭāraṇyēśvara temple at Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, a village in the Kārvēṭnagar Zamīndārī, 3 miles north-north-east of the Chinnamapēṭ Railway Station. The present name of the temple is derived from Vaṭ-āraṇya, ‘the banyan forest,’ which is the Sanskrit equivalent of Ālaṅ-gāḍu. In Tiruñāṉasambandar's Dēvāram the place is mentioned by the name Paḻaiyaṉūr-Ālaṅgāḍu, i.e. ‘Ālaṅgāḍu (near) Paḻaiyaṉūr.’ And the subjoined inscription speaks of it as “Tiruvālaṅgāḍu (near) Paḻaiyaṉūr in Paḻaiyaṉūr-nāḍu, (a subdivision) of Mēṉmalai.”Mēlmalai or (with sandhi) Mēṉmalai means ‘the Western hills.’ Paḻaiyaṉūr is found on the Madras Survey Map of the Kārvēṭnagar Zamīndārī; it is close to Tiruvālaṅgāḍu and 3 miles north-east of the Chinnamapēṭ Railway Station. According to another inscription at Tiruvālaṅgāḍu (No. 16 of 1896), Mēlmalai, the district to which Paḻaiyaṉūr-nāḍu belonged, was included in Jayaṅgoṇda Śōlamaṇḍalam.

The historical introduction and the date of this inscription are identical with those of No. 64. The inscription records that Rājēndra-Chōḷa II. issued an order to the effect that twenty-five families of Śaṅkarappāḍi should be settled on the land of Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, that the new settlement should be called Rājēndra-Śōḻappāḍi (after the name of the king), and that the settlers should have the duty of looking after fifteen lamps of the temple.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . tiru maṉṉi viḷaṅkum Irukuvaṭaṉaiya tan=toḷum vāḷun=tuṇaiyenak=keḻalar varucaṉai kaṭan=tu vayirākāttu kuñcarak=kuḻām pala vāri Añcaliccak=karakoṭṭattu tārāvaraicaṉaittik=ku nikaḻattiṟai koṇṭaruḷi Aruk=kanutaiyattācaiyiliruk=kum kamalamaṉaiya nilamakaḷtanṉai munniṉṉīrk=kuḷittavaṉṉāḷ tirumālāti
k=keḻalāki Eṭuttanna yātum caliyā vakai Initeṭuttu tan kuṭai niḻal Inpuṟa Irutti kirtiyum puliyun=ticaitoṟum celutti pukaḻun=tarumamum puvitoṟuniṟutti vīramum tiyākamumānamum karuṇaiyum Urimaiccuṟṟamāk=kippiyāttalanikaḻa cayamum tānum vīṟṟirun=tu kulamaṇimakuṭamuṟaimaiyilccūṭi taṉ kaḻal tarā
tilar cūṭacceṅkol nāvalampuvitoṟunaṭāttiya kovirājakecarivanmarāna Uṭaiyār śrīrājendracoḻadevaṟku yāṇṭu Iraṇṭāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu maṇaiyiṟkoṭ=ṭattu puricaināṭṭuccivapurattuppakalirukkaiyilttiruvamutu ceytaruḷāyiruntu meṉmalaippaḻaiya
ṉuūrnāṭṭuppaḻaiyaṉuūr tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiya mahādevarkku Ivvūr nilattile rājentracoḻappāṭipyeṉṉum piyarāl Irupattaiñcu caṅkarappāṭikkuṭi Eṟṟippatinañcu tirunan=tāviḷakkukku veṇṭum Eṇṇaiyaṭṭi Erikkappaṇṇa veṇṭumeṉṟu naṅ
kaṉmikaḷil vīracoḻappallavaraiyaṉ namakkuccoṉṉamaiyil Irupattaiñcu caṅkarappāṭikkuṭiyum Ittevarkkuttiruviḷakkeṇṇaiyaṭṭakkaṭavarkaḷāka nāme kuṭuttomeṉṟu tirumantiravo
lai Arumoḻiviḻupparayar Eḻuttiṉāṟpukun=ta tiruvāykkeḻvippaṭi Ittanmattukku Aḻivu ceyvār tiruvāṇai maṟuttāreṉṟu kal veṭṭukavenṟu Atikārikaḷ nāṅkoṟṟakkaṭampaṉ Eva Iraṅkanārāṉa vīracoḻap
pallavaraiyarkku kuṭikaḷukku kuṭi Irukkaiyum kanṟu mey pāḻum taṇṇīṟkuḷam taṭākamum cuṭukāṭum

app.note: No. 64 reads yeñcalil. Probably the reading intended in the present inscription is the same as in No. 67: Añcalil cakkarakoṭṭattu, ‘at Śakkarakōṭṭam (whose inhabitants were) fearless.’

div.p: (Line 3.) Hail! Prosperity ! In the second year (of the reign) of king Rājakēsarivarman, alias the lord Śrī-Rājēndra-Śōḻadēva, who etc.The historical introduction of this inscription is the same as in No. 64. —the following royal order was received with the signature of the royal secretary (tiru-mandirav-ōlai) Arumoḻi-Viḻupparayar:—“While (we) were dining in the day-residence (pagal-irukkai) at Śivapuram in Puriśai-nāḍu, (a subdivision) of Maṇaiyiṟkōṭṭam,This district is the same as Maṇayiṟkōṭṭam or Maṇaviṟkōṭṭam in Vol. I. p. 147. (a district) of Jayaṅgoṇḍa-Śōḻa-maṇḍalam, (and) when Vīra-Śōḻa-Pallavaraiyaṉ, (one) among our officials (kaṉmi), submitted to us that twenty-five families of Śaṅkarappāḍi should be settled on the land of this village, (that this settlement should be called) by the name of Rājēndra-Śōḻappāḍi, and that (they) should supply the oil required for, and keep burning, fifteen perpetual lamps (in the temple) of Mahādēva at Tiruvālaṅgāḍu (near) Paḻaiyaṉūr in Paḻaiyaṉūr-nāḍu, (a subdivision) of Mēṉmalai,—we granted that the twenty-five families of Śaṅkarappāḍi should supply lamp-oil to this god.”

(L. 6.) Accordingly, the magistrate (adhikārin) Nāṅgoṟṟa-Kaḍambaṉ ordered:— “Let it be engraved on stone that those who shall cause injury to this charity will have disobeyed the royal order.” . . . . . . . . . .
cuṭukāṭum . This line and the remaining two lines which are preserved are much damaged.
Digital edition of SII 3.65 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
